title: De Arte Lacrimandi
summary: The checked-in CME00093 XML is a repository copy of the University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item derived from Robert Max Garrett's complete article 'De Arte Lacrimandi,' Anglia 32 (1909), pp. 269-294. U-M retained Garrett's substantive four-paragraph preface, his 72-stanza Middle English text, manuscript-folio milestones, and apparatus. Garrett directly transcribed only British Library Harley MS 2274, historical ff. 35a-46b / current ff. 35r-46v. DIMEV 3777 adds three collateral witnesses—Bodleian MS Bodley 423, Corpus Christi College Oxford MS 261, and Trinity College Dublin MS 160—and later Baker scholarship; none is made a retroactive source of Garrett's text. Complete scans of the Anglia volume, DIMEV release, Abbott's Trinity catalog, and Saupe's supporting METS edition are cached. Digital Bodleian's complete published selection for Bodley 423 is also cached, but it contains only five images, none overlapping the poem. No publicly obtainable whole-manuscript facsimile was verified for any of the four witnesses, so whole-object request routes are recorded separately from work locators.
review_notes: The cataloged medieval work is the anonymous Middle English poem. Garrett's modern-English preface is encoded editorial paratext, not part of the poem.
Garrett's foliation 35a-46b corresponds to the current BL locator ff. 35r-46v.
Garrett's ababababbcbC makes the refrain line typographically distinct as capital C; DIMEV's ababababbcbc expresses the rhyme sounds and is not contradictory.

Bodleian's five-image object is complete only as a capture of that provider-published selection. Its images are fols. 228r, 355r, 401v, 402v, and 409v, with zero overlap with the poem at ff. 192v-205.

Garrett dated the Harley hand and language to the very end of the fifteenth or beginning of the sixteenth century; the current BL parent record dates the manuscript to 1450-1499. Both are retained as attributed claims.
DIMEV 1.0's incipit says 'My sinful language to abate,' while Garrett, CME00093, and the BL title use living/lyuyng; the discrepancy remains open.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00093
entities.description: The repository artifact consumed by the General Corpus publication workflow and obtained through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00093
entities.description: Public electronic text of Garrett's entire article, including the English editorial preface, Latin headings, 72-stanza Middle English poem, notes, printed-page breaks, historical manuscript-folio milestones, and typographic markup.

entities.label: Robert Max Garrett's De Arte Lacrimandi (1909)
entities.description: The immediate source edition: a complete Anglia article comprising Garrett's preface on pp. 269-270 and his 72-stanza transcription with notes on pp. 270-294.

bibliographic.citation: Robert Max Garrett, 'De Arte Lacrimandi,' Anglia: Zeitschrift fur englische Philologie 32, new series 20 (1909), 269-294.
bibliographic.title: De Arte Lacrimandi

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of complete Anglia 32 (1909)
entities.description: A complete 532-page scan of the bound journal volume. Garrett's article occupies PDF pages 279-304, corresponding to printed pp. 269-294.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with OCR and scan metadata

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2274, ff. 35r-46v
entities.description: Garrett's sole direct witness, a 72-stanza paper copy inserted within a composite collection of liturgical, devotional, medical, astrological, computus, and lexical material.

date_statements.label: Current BL date for the manuscript
date_statements.value: Second half of the fifteenth century

date_statements.label: Garrett's date judgment for the poem hand and language
date_statements.value: Very end of the fifteenth or beginning of the sixteenth century

holding.notes: The target component has its own exact record at https://searcharchives.bl.uk/catalog/041-002772893.

physical_description.support: Composite paper and parchment codex; the poem is on paper
physical_description.extent: 77 folios, plus five modern blank endleaves at front and four at rear; poem ff. 35r-46v
notes: The poem is written in bastard secretary according to the current component record.
Garrett reports a fine hand and marginal corrections in several hands of different periods.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 423, ff. 192v-205
entities.description: A collateral 76-stanza witness headed 'Here begynneth a tretys to lerne to wepe.' DIMEV reports five stanzas absent from Harley and printed by Baker in 1983.

physical_description.extent: Composite codex; target ff. 192v-205; part B reported as 98 folios
notes: Digital Bodleian's selected-image object supplies only generic mid-fifteenth-century/England metadata and does not image the target leaves.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian five-image selection for MS Bodley 423
entities.description: The complete published selection contains five 35mm-derived images at fols. 228r, 355r, 401v, 402v, and 409v. It is partial manuscript coverage and has no overlap with De Arte Lacrimandi at ff. 192v-205.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and five color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Corpus Christi College Oxford MS 261, fol. 101
entities.description: A collateral fragment of three stanzas, including the refrain. The exact physical date, support, and current item-level catalog description were not verified from a publicly accessible institutional record.

holding.notes: The witness locus is supplied by DIMEV and Baker's article title, not a currently verified exact College catalog record.

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin MS 160, part I, ff. 1r-13v
entities.description: A collateral 52-stanza copy identified by Abbott as 'The Virgin Mary's Tale' and by its recurring 'Who cannot wepe, cum lerne at me' refrain.

holding.notes: The current exact online catalog record did not resolve during review; the shelfmark and work locus are supported by Abbott and DIMEV.

physical_description.extent: Target part I, ff. 1r-13v; whole codex extent not established from the sources consulted
notes: Abbott groups the poem with later English verse and Peter Idley's Instructions.

entities.label: DIMEV 1.0 record 3777 / NIMEV 2347
entities.description: The modern electronic witness census and bibliography for the work, listing four witnesses, their local extents and titles, stanza/refrain form, and Garrett and Baker publications.

entities.label: D. C. Baker's edition of the Corpus 261 fragment (1969)
entities.description: A later article devoted to the three-stanza Corpus Christi College Oxford MS 261 fragment; it is collateral scholarship, not a source of Garrett's 1909 edition.

bibliographic.citation: D. C. Baker, '‘Therefor to Wepe, Cum Lerne Off Me’: A Planctus Fragment in MS. Corpus Christi College, Oxford F.261,' Medium Aevum 38 (1969), 291-294.
bibliographic.title: 'Therefor to Wepe, Cum Lerne Off Me': A Planctus Fragment in MS. Corpus Christi College, Oxford F.261

entities.label: D. C. Baker's De Arte Lacrimandi supplement and corrections (1983)
entities.description: A later scholarly supplement that prints five Bodley 423 stanzas absent from Harley and supplies corrections and expanded witness information. It is not upstream of Garrett or CME00093.

bibliographic.citation: D. C. Baker, '‘De Arte Lacrimandi’: A Supplement and Some Corrections, Medium Aevum 52.2 (1983), 222-226.
bibliographic.title: 'De Arte Lacrimandi': A Supplement and Some Corrections

entities.label: T. K. Abbott's Catalogue of the Manuscripts in Trinity College Dublin (1900)
entities.description: A complete historical printed catalog; entry 160 identifies the paper codex, broad date, and 'The Virgin Mary's Tale' by its refrain.

bibliographic.citation: T. K. Abbott, Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Trinity College, Dublin (Dublin: Hodges, Figgis & Co.; London: Longmans, Green & Co., 1900).
bibliographic.title: Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Trinity College, Dublin

entities.label: Karen Saupe's Middle English Marian Lyrics (1997)
entities.description: A modern METS anthology whose commentary to a distinct shorter same-refrain lyric cites Garrett and summarizes De Arte Lacrimandi. It is supporting comparison, not an edition of CME00093.

bibliographic.citation: Karen Saupe, ed., Middle English Marian Lyrics (Kalamazoo, MI: Medieval Institute Publications, 1997).
bibliographic.title: Middle English Marian Lyrics

agents.name: Digital Index of Middle English Verse

assertion.notes: Garrett names no other source witness.

subject.description: All five images in the Digital Bodleian object

scope.notes: The selection has zero overlap with the poem at ff. 192v-205.

locators: within ff. 192v-205; exact folios not verified

object.description: MS 160, including The Virgin Mary's Tale

scope.notes: Saupe's main edited lyric is a distinct shorter poem with the same refrain.

access.access_method: Repository checkout or pinned public GitHub blob
Public Internet Archive item and direct files

local_copies.label: Complete Anglia 32 volume PDF
notes: Complete coverage of the printed volume; the work portion is printed pp. 269-294 / PDF pp. 279-304.

access.format: PDF, OCR text, and scan metadata

access.access_method: Public parent and exact component catalog records; no Digitised Content link is exposed
notes: Public catalog access is not manuscript-image access.

access.access_method: British Library online Digitisation Services order, subject to condition, feasibility, quote, payment, and separate reuse terms
access.format: Quote-dependent original-item color photography or surrogate scan
notes: Request the complete physical object: boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all 77 manuscript folios recto and verso, modern endleaves, edges and detached matter when offered; identify the poem at ff. 35r-46v separately.
Ask for color photography from the original rather than black-and-white surrogate microfilm where feasible.

local_copies.label: Complete five-image Digital Bodleian published selection
notes: Contains every canvas in the provider's five-image object at an approximately 1800-pixel access profile.
The pictured leaves are fols. 228r, 355r, 401v, 402v, and 409v; none overlaps De Arte Lacrimandi at ff. 192v-205.

access.format: Five IIIF JPEG images from 35mm slides

access.access_method: Order missing images through Bodleian Imaging Services; request the entire physical composite rather than only the poem
notes: Request boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf recto and verso, edges and detached matter when offered; map the poem at ff. 192v-205 separately.

access.access_method: Email Corpus Christi College archives to ask whether an existing microfilm or complete digital surrogate is available and request a whole-object quote

local_copies.label: CCC application for permission to use images

access.format: Existing microfilm/digital scan or new photography, subject to availability and quote
notes: The College states that special collections are closed to readers and access extremely limited until August 2027; research and reprographic responses may be delayed.
Request the complete physical manuscript and binding matter, identifying the three-stanza work fragment at f. 101 separately.

access.access_method: Ordinary reader access is temporarily closed during the College's library project
notes: The current notice anticipates closure until August 2027 and says access is extremely limited; this is a dated operational status, not a permanent denial.

access.access_method: Public complete Internet Archive scan and derivatives

local_copies.label: Complete Abbott 1900 catalog PDF
notes: MS 160 is printed p. 21 / PDF p. 54.

access.format: PDF, OCR, scan-data XML, and metadata

access.access_method: Independent researchers may apply for a visiting reader card and request manuscripts at least one week in advance
access.format: Onsite consultation; self-photography may be permitted for personal research

access.access_method: Submit the Digital Collections image request form through Research Collections; allow at least three weeks and obtain a separate publication license where required

local_copies.label: TCD Digital Collections image request form

access.format: High-resolution TIFF or JPEG subject to condition and approval
notes: Request the entire current physical object, including covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf, edges and detached matter when offered; map the poem separately at part I, ff. 1r-13v.
The 2023 list states a EUR 25 setup/first-page fee plus EUR 2 per additional image, VAT where applicable, and separate republication-license fees; obtain a current quotation.

access.access_method: World Microfilms lists TCD Literature reel 7 as including MS 160, 'Poem in English: The Virgin Mary's Tale'
access.format: Microfilm reel; exact whole-codex and work coverage unverified
notes: The vendor listing proves a purchasable surrogate route but not whether the reel reproduces the entire manuscript or only part of it.

access.access_method: Public record page and complete Zenodo data release
JSTOR stable article page or institutional library access; no verified public full text
Official Medium Aevum issue metadata links JSTOR 43628733; no verified public full text
Public METS reading interface and complete downloadable edition PDF

local_copies.label: Complete METS Middle English Marian Lyrics edition PDF
notes: The supporting note about De Arte Lacrimandi is printed p. 221 / PDF p. 224; the main edited text is a different same-refrain lyric.

rights.component: Anonymous medieval textual content of De Arte Lacrimandi
rights.basis: The anonymous work and all four surviving manuscript copies predate modern copyright by centuries. This statement does not cover modern editions, transcriptions, photographs, metadata, or provider contracts.
The U-M item presents the public historical text under its collection-level No Copyright-United States framework; this does not grant rights in manuscript images or later scholarship.

rights.component: Garrett's 1909 article, transcription, preface, and apparatus
rights.basis: The article was published in 1909 and is in the United States public domain by age. No worldwide status is asserted.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan, OCR, and metadata delivery
rights.basis: The scanned 1909 volume is public-domain in the United States; Internet Archive access does not itself create a worldwide open license or override provider terms for site use and metadata.

rights.component: DIMEV 1.0 source data, documentation, and repository contents
rights.basis: The downloaded release's LICENSE and README state Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: Five Digital Bodleian images and their IIIF delivery
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license subject to provider caveats
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest attribution identifies Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0; the provider terms require attribution and noncommercial use and warn that item-specific restrictions may apply.

rights.component: Future images supplied by Bodleian Imaging Services
rights.basis: Current Imaging Services materials state that scans of out-of-copyright material are supplied under CC BY-NC 4.0 and that commercial or non-academic reproduction may require separate permission and fees.

rights.component: Images supplied by Corpus Christi College Oxford
rights.basis: The College's downloaded conditions assert image copyright, require advance permission for publication and online use, limit permissions by edition/use, and may impose fees.

rights.component: Images supplied by Trinity College Dublin Digital Collections
rights.basis: TCD's form and price list separate image supply from republication licensing, quote territory/language fees, require a signed license, and reserve approval based on rights and conservation review.
The catalog was published in 1900 and is in the United States public domain by age; Internet Archive access and metadata remain separate provider layers.

rights.component: Saupe's 1997 edition PDF, editorial text, and commentary
rights.basis: METS makes the digital edition available for individual use and asks users to contact the Executive Director to reprint digital text. Its separately downloadable XML license must not be transferred to the PDF without component-specific evidence.

Caching records research access and checksum provenance only. It does not establish a right to redistribute provider files, manuscript images, modern editions, or request forms through Git or publication packages.

editorial_practices.description: U-M encoded Garrett's complete article, not only the medieval poem: four prose preface paragraphs on pp. 269-270 precede the poem on pp. 270-294.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Harley manuscript text and marginal corrections in several hands
editorial_practices.representation: Garrett records manuscript corrections, supralinear additions, omissions, and alternative readings in 36 encoded footnotes and occasional brackets
editorial_practices.description: The apparatus distinguishes a range of Harley corrections and editorial supplies, but Garrett gives no full diplomatic editorial policy.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Garrett's 72 italic spans, usually letters or short sequences within words and one recurring italic '7' sign
editorial_practices.representation: U-M preserves these as HI REND='italic' but supplies no ABBR elements or manuscript letterforms
editorial_practices.description: The placement strongly suggests that many italics mark editorial expansions or supplied characters, but neither Garrett's preface nor the XML states one universal function. The exact manuscript forms require facsimile comparison.
notes: Do not reconstruct manuscript abbreviations solely from the normalized XML typography.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00093.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00093 and preserves Garrett's complete article, headings, preface, 72-stanza poem, notes, page breaks, historical foliation, italics, and superscript markup.

evidence.citation: Reproducible structural and element audit of CME00093.xml.
evidence.summary: Counts 1 DIV1, 2 DIV2, 74 HEAD, 72 LG, 864 L, 4 P, 26 PB, 23 folio MILESTONE, 36 NOTE, 72 italic HI, 12 SUP, and no ABBR; confirms article coverage from printed pp. 269-294.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, De Arte Lacrimandi, CME00093, and CME collection context.
evidence.summary: Provides the public electronic text, stable identifier, collection context, and U-M access/rights layer.

evidence.citation: Robert Max Garrett, 'De Arte Lacrimandi,' Anglia 32 (1909), complete volume scan.
evidence.summary: Primary immediate-edition evidence for publication facts, complete article boundary, the Harley-only source statement, historical foliation, physical and date observations, stanza/rhyme account, poem text, apparatus, and Garrett's closing signature.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for angliazeitschrif32tbuoft.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete 532-page scan item and exact derivative files cached for research.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2274 and component ff. 35r-46v.
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark, composite contents, paper/parchment support, 77-folio extent plus modern endleaves, dimensions, second-half-fifteenth-century date, target component, 72 twelve-line stanzas, refrain, script, and absence of exposed Digitised Content.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services, online order form, Imaging Services terms, and rate card.
evidence.summary: Documents the current order route, required shelfmark/folio/format details, rare-item expert photography, color-request instruction, price basis, and separate reproduction-permission layer.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse 1.0, record 3777 / NIMEV 2347 and complete data release.
evidence.summary: Modern census for four witnesses, locators, local titles, stanza counts, refrain and rhyme, Garrett/Baker bibliography, five Bodley-only stanzas, and CC BY 4.0 release terms.

evidence.citation: Birgitta in England, Digital Birgitta, Bodleian Library MS Bodley 423, part B.
evidence.summary: Assigns composite part B to England and approximately 1430-1440 and lists the target incipit among its contents. It is retained as scholarly part-level evidence, not presented as the Bodleian catalog.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF object for MS Bodley 423.
evidence.summary: Exact manifest and complete cached inventory for the provider's five-image 35mm selection, its composite/mid-fifteenth-century metadata, CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution, and zero target-work overlap.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Explains that most exact Digital Bodleian items use CC BY-NC 4.0, requires item-level checks and attribution, and distinguishes noncommercial academic reuse from commercial use.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services, order images and reproduce images.
evidence.summary: Provides current image-order prices and turnaround, a route for items absent from Digital Bodleian, and CC BY-NC/permission distinctions for supplied images and publication.

evidence.citation: D. C. Baker, '‘Therefor to Wepe, Cum Lerne Off Me’: A Planctus Fragment in MS. Corpus Christi College, Oxford F.261, Medium Aevum 38 (1969), 291-294, doi:10.2307/43627564.
evidence.summary: Exact bibliographic and stable-record evidence for Baker's treatment of the Corpus 261 fragment; full text was not publicly retrieved, so claims are limited to the article title, citation, and DIMEV's witness entry.

evidence.citation: D. C. Baker, '‘De Arte Lacrimandi’: A Supplement and Some Corrections, Medium Aevum 52.2 (1983), 222-226.
evidence.summary: Official issue metadata confirms author, title, pages, year, and JSTOR identifier; DIMEV specifies that pp. 223-225 print five Bodley stanzas absent from Harley.

evidence.citation: Corpus Christi College Oxford, Special and Historic Collections and contact pages.
evidence.summary: Provides archives contact details and the dated notice that reader access is closed and research/reprographic capacity extremely limited until August 2027.

evidence.citation: Corpus Christi College Oxford, Digitisation and Imaging, fees/conditions, and image-permission application.
evidence.summary: Documents availability enquiries for existing microfilm/digital scans, quote-dependent new images, College-asserted image copyright, advance publication permission, use-limited licenses, and fees.

evidence.citation: T. K. Abbott, Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Trinity College, Dublin (1900), entry 160.
evidence.summary: Historical catalog evidence for MS 160's paper support, broad fifteenth-sixteenth-century date, English verse contents, and target title/refrain.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Dublin Library Research Collections guide, visit planning, and services.
evidence.summary: Confirms independent-researcher access, visitor application and advance manuscript requests, contacts, self-photography for personal research, and separate Imaging Services/publication requirements.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Dublin Digital Collections services, 2023 price list, and image request form.
evidence.summary: Documents quote/order contacts, formats, minimum processing time, per-image fees, VAT, republication licenses, conservation review, and the Library's right to decline imaging.

evidence.citation: World Microfilms, Trinity College Dublin manuscript reels.
evidence.summary: Lists Literature reel 7 as including MS 160, 'Poem in English: The Virgin Mary's Tale'; the page does not establish whether the reel covers the whole codex.

evidence.citation: Karen Saupe, ed., Middle English Marian Lyrics (1997), commentary to section 39.
evidence.summary: Supporting modern commentary distinguishes a shorter same-refrain lyric from Garrett's much longer Harley poem and summarizes the latter as Mary's life from birth through Assumption.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright & Citation.
evidence.summary: States that digital editions are for individual use and directs reprint requests to the Executive Director; separately licenses METS XML as CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, which is not generalized to the downloadable PDF.

open_questions.question: Will the British Library supply or publish a complete color facsimile of Harley MS 2274, and what redistribution terms would attach?
next_steps: Submit the whole-object Digitisation Services request with all binding and leaf surfaces specified.
Identify the poem at ff. 35r-46v separately and request color from the original where feasible.
Obtain written permission terms before placing supplied images in Git or print-on-demand outputs.

open_questions.question: Can a complete color facsimile of Bodleian MS Bodley 423 be obtained when the public object exposes only five non-overlapping leaves?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian Imaging Services whether a complete existing surrogate or new whole-object order is feasible.
Request all composite and binding surfaces and map the poem at ff. 192v-205 separately.
Compare Baker's five additional stanzas against the complete images.

open_questions.question: Do complete existing digital or microfilm surrogates of Corpus Christi College Oxford MS 261 and Trinity College Dublin MS 160 exist, and what exactly do they cover?
next_steps: Ask Corpus archives whether World Microfilms, Bodleian, or the College can supply an existing complete MS 261 surrogate and quote new whole-object imaging if not.
Ask TCD to confirm whether Literature reel 7 covers all of MS 160 and quote a complete color object if it does not.
Map the work only after folio labels and image inventories are verified.

open_questions.question: What manuscript forms do Garrett's 72 printed italic spans represent: expanded abbreviations, supplied letters, special signs, or more than one editorial function?
next_steps: Compare every italic span against a color facsimile of Harley ff. 35r-46v.
Check whether Garrett documented his conventions elsewhere or in surviving papers.
Do not infer original letterforms from CME HI markup alone.

open_questions.question: Is DIMEV 1.0's 'My sinful language to abate' a transcription/data error for 'My sinful living to abate'?
next_steps: Inspect all four witnesses and Baker's articles.
Submit a DIMEV correction with witness evidence if appropriate.

open_questions.question: How are the 76-, 72-, 52-, and three-stanza copies textually related, and which readings reflect authorial development, scribal adaptation, or loss?
next_steps: Obtain complete surrogates and collate all four witnesses.
Review Baker 1969 and 1983 in full and current scholarship before proposing a stemma.

open_questions.question: Which manuscript-image and modern-edition files may be redistributed in the public Git repository or commercial/noncommercial print-on-demand outputs?
next_steps: Keep all source caches Git-ignored and publish links/locators only unless a component-specific license supports redistribution.
Treat Bodleian CC BY-NC images as noncommercial and verify each exact object; obtain separate written permissions from BL, Corpus, and TCD for supplied images.
Do not transfer METS XML's license to its PDF or commentary.
